query: Mexico CURP mobile phone registration 2026 CRT padron telefonia movil June latest numbers registered lines
answer:
The agent found comprehensive data from existing research files. Let me compile this into a thorough report.

---

# Mexico CURP Mobile Phone Registration (Padrón de Telefonía Móvil) — Research Report

## Overview

Mexico's current mobile phone registration program requires all mobile lines to be linked to a **CURP** (Clave Única de Registro de Población) by **June 30, 2026**. This is administered by the **CRT** (Comisión Reguladora de Telecomunicaciones), which replaced the IFT as Mexico's telecom regulator in October 2025. The program was enacted January 9, 2026.

This is Mexico's **third attempt** at a mobile phone registry, following the failed RENAUT (2008–2012) and the unconstitutional PANAUT (2021–2022). Unlike the PANAUT, the current program does **not** require biometric data — only CURP linkage — and uses decentralized data storage at each carrier.

---

## Latest Registration Numbers (as of June 1–2, 2026)

| Date | Lines Registered | Total Lines | % Registered |
|------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|
| Early April 2026 | ~26–27 million | ~161.7 million | ~16–17% |
| April 19, 2026 | ~30.2 million | ~158 million | ~19.1% |
| May 13–22, 2026 | ~48–49.5 million | ~161.7 million | ~30–31% |
| May 27, 2026 | ~51.6 million | ~161.7 million | ~32–33% |
| **June 1–2, 2026** | **~69.7 million** | **~161.7 million** | **~43%** |

- **~92 million lines remain unregistered** (~57%)
- At the current pace, ~1.5 million lines per day would need to be registered to meet the deadline — a rate analysts consider unachievable
- Telecom analyst **Ernesto Piedras** publicly stated: *"Registration of 161.7 million telephone lines is not going to be achieved."*

### By Carrier (mid-May 2026)
- **AT&T**: ~29% registered
- **Bait (Grupo Walmart)**: ~28%
- **Telcel (América Móvil)**: ~19%
- **Movistar (Telefónica)**: ~16%

---

## Deadline and Extension Status

The CRT has been **categorical** that no extension (prórroga) will be granted:

- **Ricardo Castañeda** (CRT Director General de Política Regulatoria), May 28, 2026: *"Es un plazo legal, está establecido en la ley. No lo tenemos sobre la mesa... No hay discusión alguna."*
- **Norma Solano** (CRT Comisionada Presidenta) confirmed no extension on April 21, 2026
- The CRT emphasizes only **Congress** has authority to modify the deadline
- Telecom industry (including GSMA and América Móvil) formally petitioned for an extension in January 2026; the CRT rejected it

---

## Penalties for Non-Compliance (Starting July 1, 2026)

- Unregistered lines will be **suspended** (no calls, SMS, or data)
- Emergency services (911) and seismic alerts remain available
- Suspension is administrative, not permanent cancellation
- Service restorable at any time upon completing registration
- Users do **not** lose phone numbers or balances
- **Telcel and AT&T** have already proactively disconnected ~1 million unregistered lines (as of May 2026)

---

## Legal and Historical Context

- **RENAUT (2008–2012)**: First attempt; ~30–50% compliance, never enforced, database destroyed in 2012
- **PANAUT (2021–2022)**: Required biometrics; declared **unconstitutional** by SCJN in April 2022 before implementation
- **Current program (2026)**: CURP-only, no biometrics, designed to avoid SCJN constitutional objections
- Some citizens are seeking **amparos** (injunctions), but the 2025 Amparo Law reform bars general injunctions against telecom regulations
- Total infrastructure cost: ~4,053 million pesos (~$220.7 million USD)
- **President Sheinbaum** has publicly backed the program

---

## Data Vintage Note

All data sourced from research files dated June 2, 2026 (one day old as of June 3, 2026). Live web search tools were unavailable to check for developments in the last 24 hours.

key_facts: As of June 1-2, 2026, approximately 69.7 million of 161.7 million total mobile lines in Mexico have been registered with CURP (~43%)
Approximately 92 million lines (~57%) remain unregistered with a June 30, 2026 deadline
The CRT (Comisión Reguladora de Telecomunicaciones), which replaced the IFT in October 2025, administers the program
The CRT has categorically stated no extension (prórroga) will be granted; only Congress can modify the deadline
Registration began January 9, 2026; the program requires CURP linkage only (no biometric data)
This is Mexico's third mobile registration attempt after RENAUT (2008-2012, failed enforcement) and PANAUT (2021-2022, declared unconstitutional by SCJN)
Unregistered lines will be suspended starting July 1, 2026 — no calls, SMS, or data, but 911 and seismic alerts remain available
Telcel and AT&T have already proactively disconnected approximately 1 million unregistered lines as of May 2026
By carrier compliance (mid-May): AT&T ~29%, Bait ~28%, Telcel ~19%, Movistar ~16%
Telecom industry (GSMA, América Móvil) petitioned for extension in January 2026; CRT rejected it
Infrastructure cost estimated at 4,053 million pesos (~$220.7 million USD)
Analyst Ernesto Piedras stated full registration of 161.7 million lines will not be achieved
The 2025 Amparo Law reform bars general injunctions against telecom regulations, limiting judicial challenge routes
President Claudia Sheinbaum has publicly backed the registration program
